title: Font + source license/provenance gate (blocking)
context: handwriting-practice must ensure every font and stroke-order source used in generated sheets is openly licensed (OFL, PD/CC0, CC-BY, or CC-BY-SA with share-alike handling). Without a formal, default-deny provenance gate that fails the build on any unverified asset, a proprietary glyph or unattributed source could silently slip into redistributed PDFs — a license breach that would undermine the entire project's legal standing. The gate is a blocking role: no script ships until it passes.
objective: Design and publish the font + source license/provenance gate: a documented policy (allowed/excluded license types), a build check that default-denies any asset lacking a complete verified open-license record, a required license-snapshot format (local copy + SHA-256 + Wayback URL), and OFL/CC-BY-SA/NC handling rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Policy document enumerates allowed licenses (OFL, CC0/PD, CC-BY, CC-BY-SA with SA handling) and excluded ones (proprietary, no-embed/no-redistribution, NC-only).

Default-deny rule: an asset passes only if an open license permitting redistribution (and derivatives where the model overlays/modifies) is recorded with a cited URL; missing or unparseable evidence causes FAIL with no default-allow.
License-snapshot format specified: a committed local copy of the license text, a SHA-256 hash, and a Wayback Machine URL per asset.
OFL Reserved Font Name rule encoded: overlay must remain a separate layer; any actual outline modification triggers a rename per OFL.
CC-BY-SA share-alike handling encoded: SA-derived models kept separable and SA-licensed; NC sources excluded outright.
Gate produces a committed PASS/FAIL artifact per asset and a CI check that fails the build on any unverified referenced asset.
output: A gate-policy document committed to the repo (e.g. docs/provenance-gate.md or packages/provenance-gate/README.md) plus the CI check implementation, with a PASS/FAIL artifact schema and snapshot format specified.
